LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT ANDREW ZACKS' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 PER CHALLENGED TO JUDGE BUSCH. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDREW M. ZACK'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED IN PART, ON THE CONDUCT OR ADVISE OF MCCLOSKEY, CENTRAL TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS THE CONDUCT OF COUNSEL AND THEIR CLIENT IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CASES, IN PARTICULAR, THAT THE JUDGMENT WAS OBTAINED BY "EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE" (AMENDED COMPLAINT PARAGRAPHS 9, 14 AND 15). PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HER CONTENTION THAT SHE WAS NOT SERVED. SHE RELIES ONLY ON THE MCCLOSKEY DECLARATION THAT PLAINTIFF FOLLOWED COUNSEL'S ADVISE. THAT IS NOT SUFFICIENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Law Office of Kaplan & Sam (415) 447-8300 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht (415) 956-8100, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part. Requested amount reduced by $576 for duplicate entry and errors. Defendant awarded reasonable attorney fees in the amount of $14,000. See Gilbert vs. Master Washer & Stamping Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App. 4th 212. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2007 Off Calendar

Defendant Zacks, Utrecht & Leadbetter, P.C.'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Challenge To Judge Busch)
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter, P.C.'S Special Motion To Strike (To 1st Amended Complaint).
APPEARANCES: Michael McCloskey, Esq. 771-6174 for pltf & opposing party; James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter 956-8100 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED IN PART, ON THE CONDUCT OR ADVISE OF MCCLOSKEY, CENTRAL TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS THE CONDUCT OF COUNSEL AND THEIR CLIENT IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CASES, IN PARTICULAR, THAT THE JUDGMENT WAS OBTAINED BY "EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE" (AMENDED COMPLAINT PARAGRAPHS 9, 14 AND 15). PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HER CONTENTION THAT SHE WAS NOT SERVED. SHE RELIES ONLY ON THE MCCLOSKEY DECLARATION THAT PLAINTIFF FOLLOWED COUNSEL'S ADVISE. THAT IS NOT SUFFICIENT. Order signed in open court. Court denied plaintiff's request for continuance. (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
